title: Ultrafast Sliding Ferroelectric Switching in Bilayer Hexagonal Boron Nitride Revealed by Deep Learning Molecular Dynamics
authors: Yinan Wang, Poyen Chen, Teruyasu Mizoguchi
abstract: Sliding ferroelectricity in bilayer hexagonal boron nitride (h-BN) offers compelling prospects for next-generation non-volatile memory, yet the atomistic dynamics of electric-field-driven polarization switching remain poorly understood. Here, we present a fully data-driven, coupled atomistic framework that integrates a fine-tuned MACE machine learning potential (MLP) with an equivariant graph convolutional neural network (EGCNN) for real-time Born effective charge (BEC) prediction, enabling large-scale non-equilibrium molecular dynamics simulations of AB-stacked bilayer h-BN under applied electric fields. By implementing a rigorous real-space path-integral polarization formalism combined with a state-constrained Gaussian convolution background extraction procedure, we successfully isolate the intrinsic spontaneous polarization from the dominant dielectric background. Our simulations reveal that coherent single-domain rigid sliding, completing within 5 ps, constitutes a physically viable ultrafast switching mechanism, and reproduces clean ferroelectric hysteresis loops whose shape is qualitatively consistent with experimental observations.
summary: This paper presents a deep learning-based molecular dynamics approach to simulate the ultrafast switching of ferroelectricity in bilayer h-BN. It successfully isolates intrinsic polarization from field-induced dielectric noise, revealing a picosecond-scale sliding mechanism. This work provides a powerful tool for studying the non-equilibrium dynamics of 2D sliding ferroelectrics.
main_problem: Understanding the atomistic, real-time dynamics of electric-field-driven polarization switching in sliding ferroelectrics and decoupling intrinsic polarization from dielectric background.
main_result: The study reveals an ultrafast (5 ps) coherent single-domain rigid sliding mechanism in bilayer h-BN and develops a method to extract clean ferroelectric hysteresis loops from massive dielectric backgrounds.
method: A coupled deep learning molecular dynamics framework integrating a fine-tuned MACE machine learning potential with an Equivariant Graph Convolutional Neural Network (EGCNN) for real-time Born effective charge prediction.
model_or_system: Bilayer hexagonal boron nitride (h-BN) in AB and BA stacking configurations, modeled using large-scale non-equilibrium molecular dynamics under applied out-of-plane electric fields.
key_observables: Out-of-plane polarization (Pz), Born effective charge (BEC) tensors, potential energy surfaces, and ferroelectric hysteresis loops.
important_parameters: Electric field strength (up to +/- 2.0 V/A), temperature (50 K to 300 K), and switching timescale (~5 ps).
main_assumptions: The bilayer is treated as a free-standing system (no substrate interference) and the lattice is assumed to be defect-free.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows phonon dispersions, potential energy surfaces, and the minimum energy pathway; Figure 3 illustrates atomic trajectories and RMSD during switching; Figure 4 demonstrates the extraction of intrinsic polarization from the dielectric background.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of switching dynamics, presents the MACE-EGCNN machine learning framework, validates the model accuracy against DFT, details the polarization extraction protocol, describes the observed switching mechanism and its electromechanical nature, and concludes with stability analysis of the AB state.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
